title: ranunculus oreganus subsp. macounii
intro: Ranunculus oreganus subsp. macounii, also known as Macoun's buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the ranunculaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Macoun's buttercup include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically harvested and used to make teas, tinctures, and salves that can be applied topically to the affected area.
